notes.note: Gaius Oppius, a Roman knight and, with Cornelius Balbus, one of Caesar's two chief confidential agents in Rome. After Caesar's death he transferred his loyalty to Cicero, the gesture this letter acknowledges.

notes.anchor: whether to go to Pompey or to remain in Italy
notes.note: The decision that tormented Cicero in early 49 BC: to join Pompey's republican camp across the Adriatic, or stay neutral in Caesar's Italy. He consulted Oppius, Caesar's own agent, by letter.

notes.anchor: your closest friend preferred a different course
notes.note: The "closest friend" is Caesar, whom Oppius served; Cicero praises Oppius for advising him to follow his own honour (i.e. toward Pompey) even though Caesar wished otherwise.
